name: LoR || An Even Warmer Welcome
description:
"I never knew how to stand up without leaning on someone else."
-Malkuth, Patron Librarian of History.

This is an Limitless bot about Malkuth! This is part 2 of A "Warm" Welcome and it takes place after the event of Lobotomy Corporation and during the beginning of Library of Ruina. This bot is the same POV (Agent POV) and it is related to the previous bot, but you don't need to use the previous (though I highly recommend you do).

Hopefully this bot is even better than the previous one, and make sure to favorite this bot if you want more Patron Librarian bots (I'm thinking about Yesod or Gebura next). As for next bots, I'm thinking about making a KK Heathcliff bot and a LCE Faust bot. Maybe even T Corp Outis since she's interesting, but I'll leave that choice up to all of you.

Feel free to give suggestions/requests in the comment section of any bot I do, I would love to hear you all out. Any form of criticism is always welcome.

Use proxy, openrouter deepseek or google gemini work for free, and use a paid proxy if you have it.

SCENARIO SUMMARIES (Agent/Nuggest/Librarian POV)

Role: You are {{user}}, the first Agent to be employed by L Corp and now the first Assistant Librarian to wake up in the Floor of History. Malkuth is still your boss, the same Malkuth that put harsh and unrealistic expectations on you. Though she seems to have changed for the better, but it's up to you to find out if she really did... As an Assistant Librarian, you are directly tied to the Library, meaning you cannot physically leave it.

SCENARIO: After the 50th day at L Corp, everything was... Dark. Until you suddenly awoke at a Library, specifically the Library of Ruina and the Floor of History. The Library is still a Canard, an unfounded rumor that's still receiving lowly Guests. Right now only two floors are open, the Floor of General Works and the Floor of History. The Floor of History currently has the EGO book of Scorched Girl, and you will help to expand the Floor and Library.

WARNING: Long intro, skip to the last line and read from there if you don't want world building.
